seoTitle: How to Use the Table Component in Microsoft Loop
metaDescription: Build and configure a live, multiplayer data table in Microsoft Loop, including custom columns, resizing, and sharing it into Teams or Outlook.
intro: Static spreadsheets and fragmented version control make it hard to keep a team's data current. This guide covers building a Loop table component, customizing its columns, and sharing it as a live, editable block in Teams or Outlook.

steps.heading: Insert the table component
steps.body: Move your cursor to a free line in your Loop page and type /table to drop a dynamic data matrix onto the canvas.

steps.heading: Configure your columns
steps.body: Click any column header to rename it, and use the plus buttons to add new data fields.

steps.heading: Set column data types
steps.body: Click the column type dropdown to change cells into date pickers, number fields, or status labels as needed.

steps.heading: Resize and reorder as needed
steps.body: Drag column borders to resize widths and reorder your grid sequence to match how your team scans the data.

steps.heading: Create a shareable component
steps.body: Click the six-dot handle on the table's border and choose 'Create Component' to prepare it for external sharing.

steps.heading: Embed it elsewhere
steps.body: Copy the component link and paste it into Teams or Outlook to allow real-time multiplayer editing outside the original page.

faq.question: Can multiple people edit a Loop table at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, Loop's table component supports real-time multiplayer synchronization so multiple people can edit rows simultaneously.

faq.question: What column types are available in a Loop table?
faq.answer: You can set columns to plain text, numbers, date pickers, or status labels depending on the kind of data you're tracking.

faq.question: Can I use the same table in both a Loop page and a Teams chat?
faq.answer: Yes, creating a component and sharing its link lets the same live table appear and stay synced across a Loop page, Teams chat, and Outlook email.

faq.question: Can I export a Loop table's data for external reporting?
faq.answer: You can use the block options menu to lock or export table data for use outside the collaborative environment.
